title: Isakki Swamigal
meta_description: Isakki Swamigal (1917–1991), also known as Panchamukha Swamigal, was a healer and devotee of Arunachala famous for his angapradakshina and deep connection with the five-peaked Panchamukha Darshanam on the north-east side of the hill.
body_html:
Isakki Swamigal, also known as Panchamukha Swamigal, belonged to Tirunelveli District. He was born on May 2, 1917 to father Chella Perumal Pillai and mother Muthammal. From his early days, he exhibited strong devotional thoughts and maintained a consistent spiritual practice.

At the age of 13, whilst still a schoolboy, he journeyed with his parents to Tiruvannamalai to attend the Karthigai Deepam Festival. On circumambulation of Arunachala, upon arriving at the Panchamukha Peaks, he had the sensation of being at the foot of Mount Kailash. From that day onwards he felt a strong attachment to Panchamukha Darshanam, and in later times spent so much time there that he became popularly known as Panchamukha Swamigal.

On the eastern side of Arunachala, the hill appears as five peak points, representing the five faces of Shiva:

  • Sadyojata — West, Earth, Creation
  • Vamadeva — North, Water, Protection
  • Aghora — South, Fire, Dissolution/Rejuvenation
  • Tatpurusha — East, Air, Concealing Grace
  • Ishana — North-East, Ether, Revealing Grace

After completing school, Isakki married and lived as a householder in Tirunelveli. Around 1959–1960 he came to Tiruvannamalai and settled with his family near Ammani Amman Gopuram. He practised as a country Doctor using herbal and traditional medicine.

Swamigal performed meditation at several places around Arunachala. It is believed that at the northside near the Kavasa Theertham, Lord Annamalaiyar showered His blessings upon Swamigal and gave him the power of healing. He instructed all who came to him to surrender to Lord Siva in the form of Arunachala and to perform girivalam of the Hill.

During his life, Isakki Swamigal performed Arunachala angapradakshina (pradakshina by rolling the body on the ground) 1008 times. Even today he is believed to perform girivalam twice daily in his astral form.

Swamigal's Samadhi

Isakki Swamigal reached samadhi on January 9, 1991 at the age of 74 years.

Panchamukha Darshanam

Walking along the outer giripradakshina path, about a hundred yards after the Kubera Lingam shrine, on the right side there is a shrine with five small Shiva Lingams on a raised pedestal entitled 'Arunachala Panchamukha'. The five lingams were installed comparatively recently, corresponding to the five faces of Lord Shiva in his Panchanana (five-faced) aspect.

Edited from Howard Murphet 1971

Isakki Swamigal Panchamukha Darshanam
notes: No hero image before H1. Image https://framerusercontent.com/images/Q3wfJ1tX4yb2y6jcd1fgUW9vs.png appears twice on page.
